summary: The bounded evidence supports the recorded local test PASS, the explicitly SIMULATED Review Gate, and clear separation of PASS, FAIL, NOT_RUN, and SIMULATED states. It does not support completed CI or external verification gates. This assessment relies solely on the supplied recorded evidence; no checks were independently executed.

claimAudits.rationale: The report records a PASS for the specified Node test command with exit code 0 and two tests, while the preserved output records the same command, exit code, and two passing test cases. The test source contains those two cases, and the documentation limits the result to simulated local flows.
claimAudits.recommendedNextAction: Preserve the recorded artifacts and continue describing this only as local simulated test evidence.

claimAudits.rationale: The report explicitly records CI as FAIL with no evidence and raises a HIGH NO_CI risk. The traceability and NOT_RUN documentation do not provide any CI workflow or CI-run completion record, so recorded continuous-integration execution is unsupported.
missingEvidence: A CI configuration that runs the required checks
A recorded CI run result and associated logs or artifacts
claimAudits.recommendedNextAction: Add an approved minimal CI workflow and retain its run evidence before making a CI-execution claim.

claimAudits.rationale: Both permitted records consistently disclose an APPROVED local-file gate as SIMULATED, identify the simulated reviewer, state that identity was not verified, and disclaim enterprise identity verification. This supports the locked SIMULATED boundary without turning it into an independently verified approval.
claimAudits.recommendedNextAction: Keep the gate labeled SIMULATED and require a real authorized reviewer before relying on it outside this demonstration.

claimAudits.rationale: The report and documentation visibly distinguish passing local tests, failed or absent CI, intentionally NOT_RUN external checks, and simulated project and review data. The documents also state that NOT_RUN is not PASS and avoid production, provider, deployment, scale, or compliance claims.
claimAudits.recommendedNextAction: Retain these explicit PASS, FAIL, NOT_RUN, and SIMULATED labels in future reports.

claimAudits.rationale: The report lists CI and several external checks as not run, while the gate document marks dependency, browser/accessibility, load/concurrency, real identity-provider, real payment-provider, and deployment checks as NOT_RUN. Therefore, completion evidence does not exist for every listed external verification gate.
missingEvidence: Completion records for browser and accessibility verification
Completion records for load and concurrency verification
Completion records for real identity-provider verification
Completion records for real payment-provider verification
Completion records for any applicable dependency vulnerability audit
claimAudits.recommendedNextAction: Keep all external gates at NOT_RUN until separately authorized checks produce recorded completion evidence.

nextSafeAction: Accept the local simulated test evidence only within its stated boundary; keep CI at FAIL and external gates at NOT_RUN until approved automation and external verification produce recorded evidence.
